Imaging & equipment QC

Clarity you can act on, every time.

Calibration and quality assurance for mammography, MRI, and multi-site compliance — each reading measured against a documented tolerance, each result one you can sign your name to.

Reserved for pilot outcomes — added here once verified, in the same format as a resolved measurement (e.g. site · period · result).

Verified result

Reserved for pilot outcomes — added here once verified, in the same format as a resolved measurement (e.g. site · period · result).

Why programs move to us

Audited rigor, without the bloat.

QC is only as good as the discipline behind it. We build on the protocols you already answer to — ACR and AAPM guidance, state and manufacturer requirements — and make the evidence trail effortless to produce when an inspector, or your own director, asks for it.

  • Tolerance-band thinking on every measurement, not pass/fail guesswork
  • One calibrated source of truth across every site you run
  • Findings written to be acted on — flagged early, never buried

How it works

A measurement, resolved into a finding.

  1. 01

    Establish tolerance

    Every modality gets documented limits — drawn from ACR/AAPM protocols and your manufacturer specs — so "passing" means something specific, not subjective.

  2. 02

    Capture & calibrate

    Readings are taken on a consistent cadence and checked against those limits — the noisy stream reduced to a clear in-/out-of-tolerance signal.

  3. 03

    Flag & report

    Anything drifting toward a limit is flagged before it breaches. The report is the evidence trail — ready for inspection the day it's needed.

Built on

  • ACR accreditation protocols
  • AAPM QC guidance
  • State & manufacturer requirements

What you get

  • Documented tolerance per modality
  • Inspection-ready evidence trail
  • Early-flag, multi-site overview

Have a program to bring under one roof?

Start the conversation

What a log looks like

The evidence trail, legible under pressure.

Illustrative of the data shape, not a live feed. On a phone between cases, each row becomes a card showing only what's decision-relevant.

Date Modality Measurement Reading Tolerance Status
2026-06-22 Mammography Phantom CNR 4.8 ≥ 4.0 In tolerance
2026-06-22 Mammography AEC reproducibility 3.1% ≤ 5% In tolerance
2026-06-21 MRI SNR (head coil) 182 ≥ 150 In tolerance
2026-06-21 MRI Geometric distortion 1.9 mm ≤ 2 mm Flagged
2026-06-20 MRI Center frequency drift 11 Hz ≤ 25 Hz In tolerance

Bring your QC program under one calibrated roof.

Imaging or equipment, one site or many — let's talk about what "passing" should mean for your program.
